content.text: MDM Sync with RAG Index Pipelines

The MDM source catalog and the RAG vector store must stay in sync: when a golden record changes — through a merge, split, attribute update, or retirement — every chunk in the vector store that references that record must be updated to match. Without a reliable sync pipeline, the vector store drifts from the MDM catalog, producing chunks with stale classification labels, retired golden IDs still serving results, or merged records with two competing freshness clocks.

attrs.alt: MDM sync pipeline for RAG index

content.text: Every mutation to the MDM catalog emits a typed domain event. The event schema carries enough information for the sync worker to apply the correct update to the vector store without querying the catalog again.
Retag absorbed chunks; delete duplicate vectors
Retag subset of chunks by alias match
Delete all chunks; add golden_id to retrieval block-list
Patch metadata on all chunks for golden_id
Add to retrieval block-list (do not delete chunks)
Bulk migrate all chunks with old_value to new_value
Outbox Pattern for Reliable Event Delivery

MDM writes events to an outbox table in the same transaction as the catalog mutation. A separate relay process polls the outbox and publishes events to the message bus. This guarantees that no event is lost even if the bus is temporarily unreachable at the time of the MDM write.

The sync worker consumes MDM events and applies the corresponding update to the vector store. Every handler is idempotent: running the same event twice produces the same result as running it once, allowing safe at-least-once delivery.

from __future__ import annotations
import logging
from typing import Callable, Any

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class IndexSyncWorker:
    """Handles MDM change events and applies updates to the vector store."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        vector_store,
        retrieval_block_list: set[str],
    ) -> None:
        self._vs = vector_store
        self._blocked = retrieval_block_list

    def handle(self, event_type: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        handler = {
            "source.merged":       self._on_merged,
            "source.split":        self._on_split,
            "source.retired":      self._on_retired,
            "source.attrs_changed":self._on_attrs_changed,
            "source.suspended":    self._on_suspended,
            "source.reinstated":   self._on_reinstated,
            "ref_data.changed":    self._on_ref_data_changed,
        }.get(event_type)

        if handler is None:
            log.warning("Unknown MDM event type: %s", event_type)
            return
        handler(payload)

    def _on_merged(self, p: dict) -> None:
        survivor_id = p["survivor_id"]
        absorbed_id = p["absorbed_id"]
        # Retag every chunk whose golden_id == absorbed_id → survivor_id
        count = self._vs.update_metadata_where(
            filter={"golden_id": absorbed_id},
            update={"golden_id": survivor_id},
        )
        log.info("source.merged: retagged %d chunks %s→%s", count, absorbed_id, survivor_id)
        # Remove any now-duplicate vectors (same content, two golden_ids)
        # In practice: rely on the subsequent quality scanner to flag these
        # rather than deleting proactively, to avoid data loss.

    def _on_split(self, p: dict) -> None:
        new_id = p["new_id"]
        alias_ids_to_move = set(p["alias_ids_moved_to_new"])
        count = self._vs.update_metadata_where(
            filter={"source_alias": {"$in": list(alias_ids_to_move)}},
            update={"golden_id": new_id},
        )
        log.info("source.split: retagged %d chunks to new golden_id %s", count, new_id)

    def _on_retired(self, p: dict) -> None:
        golden_id = p["golden_id"]
        self._blocked.add(golden_id)   # immediate retrieval block
        deleted = self._vs.delete_where(filter={"golden_id": golden_id})
        log.info("source.retired: blocked + deleted %d chunks for %s", deleted, golden_id)

    def _on_attrs_changed(self, p: dict) -> None:
        golden_id = p["golden_id"]
        changed = p["changed_fields"]   # dict of field → new_value
        count = self._vs.update_metadata_where(
            filter={"golden_id": golden_id},
            update=changed,
        )
        log.info("source.attrs_changed: patched %d chunks for %s (%s)", count, golden_id, list(changed))

    def _on_suspended(self, p: dict) -> None:
        self._blocked.add(p["golden_id"])
        log.info("source.suspended: %s added to retrieval block-list", p["golden_id"])

    def _on_reinstated(self, p: dict) -> None:
        self._blocked.discard(p["golden_id"])
        log.info("source.reinstated: %s removed from retrieval block-list", p["golden_id"])

    def _on_ref_data_changed(self, p: dict) -> None:
        field = p["field"]
        old_val = p["old_value"]
        new_val = p["new_value"]
        count = self._vs.update_metadata_where(
            filter={field: old_val},
            update={field: new_val},
        )
        log.info("ref_data.changed: migrated %d chunks %s=%s → %s", count, field, old_val, new_val)
All handlers must be safe to re-run on the same event. The key patterns are:
on a chunk that already has
because deleting already-deleted chunks returns a zero count without error.
only if the new_value in the event is the authoritative final state. Events must carry the target value, not a delta.
at the consumer level: the sync worker records the last processed
per event type and skips duplicates within a 24-hour window.

The event-driven sync handles real-time changes, but it cannot catch gaps caused by lost messages, consumer restarts, or changes made directly to the catalog outside the normal event path. A daily reconciliation job performs a full comparison between the MDM catalog and the vector store metadata.
Outbox relay retry; retrieval block-list applied immediately in-process
Idempotent retag on retry; reconciliation job catches stragglers
Chunks with stale classification or permissions
Reconciliation job detects and re-emits patch event
Reconciliation job detects mismatch and re-emits split event
Old content_class value still present in chunks
Full bulk migration; reconciliation job verifies completion

Keeping the RAG vector store in sync with the MDM catalog requires three complementary mechanisms: an outbox pattern for reliable event delivery from the catalog, an idempotent sync worker that applies retags, deletes, patches, and block-list updates in response to typed MDM events, and a daily reconciliation job that catches any gaps left by lost messages or out-of-band catalog changes. Together these ensure that the access controls, classification labels, and golden identifiers that the MDM catalog maintains are consistently reflected in every chunk the retriever serves.
